notes: Three consecutive user prompts in ONE live pi REPL session, validating that state resets to ready between turns — ready→working→ready three times on the SAME rollout session_id — and that the session survives all three turns as a single live entity, then tears down once. WHY interactive (not headless `pi --print`): headless pi is single-shot and exits with process_exited after one reply (that's the basic-turn / session-end shape), so it can't show turn-to-turn state reset; the pi interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout REPL alive across all three turns via send+wait_turn and kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE process_exited, not three. WHY the 3s sleeps between wait_turn and the next send: they sit past the classifier's debounce window so each turn's working→ready flip is observed cleanly before the next prompt re-enters working; without them the next send can land before the prior turn settles and the arcs blur. All three arcs are pinned to the same session via same_session_as:session_birth so a co-occurring proc-<PID> pre-session can't satisfy a turn match. TIMING NOTE (C7): the live REPL may briefly emit a proc-<PID> pre-session born ready before the UUID-keyed rollout transcript materializes; session_birth binds the UUID rollout session (the one the turns and the pid_discovered land on), and every later phase chains off it. teardown anchors to t3_end (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-third-turn ready arc. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance) — only the structural three-turn arc, single distinct rollout session_id, single process_exited, and final ready state are stable.

text: Rollout session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
First reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Second prompt → working again on the same session
Second reply completes → back to ready
Third prompt → working again on the same session
Third reply completes → back to ready
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the third turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
